title: Restoration Projects Explorer
description: Discover nature-based carbon removal projects worldwide — from mangrove restoration in Indonesia to reforestation in the Amazon.

stages.title: 8,877 Projects Fighting for Our Planet
stages.body:
Across the globe, **8,877 verified carbon projects** are actively removing or avoiding CO₂ emissions. From protecting mangroves in Indonesia to building clean cookstoves in Kenya, these projects represent humanity's effort to repair what we've broken.

Each project is verified by standards like **Verra (VCS)** or the **Gold Standard**, ensuring real, measurable impact. Together, they represent **billions of tons** of CO₂ kept out of — or removed from — the atmosphere.

callouts.label: Project types
callouts.value: Renewable energy, forestry, agriculture, methane capture

stages.title: Project Spotlight
stages.description: Browse verified carbon removal projects. Click cards to flip for details.

cards.title: Tambopata-Bahuaja REDD+
cards.subtitle: Peru · Amazon Rainforest
cards.detail: Protecting 591,119 hectares of tropical rainforest in the Peruvian Amazon. Avoids 4,571,426 tCO₂e over 10 years by preventing deforestation.

cards.title: Kenya Cookstove Distribution
cards.subtitle: Kenya · Rural Communities
cards.detail: Distributing efficient cookstoves to 14,000+ households. Reduces firewood use by 50%, cutting CO₂ and improving indoor air quality.

cards.title: Indonesia Mangrove Restoration
cards.subtitle: Kalimantan · Coastal Ecosystems
cards.detail: Restoring 10,000 hectares of degraded mangrove forests. Mangroves sequester 3-5x more carbon per hectare than terrestrial forests.

cards.title: India Wind Power Project
cards.subtitle: Tamil Nadu · Clean Energy
cards.detail: 42 wind turbines generating 80 MW, displacing coal-fired electricity. Provides clean energy to 250,000 homes.

cards.title: Congo Basin Peatlands
cards.subtitle: DRC · Tropical Wetlands
cards.detail: Protecting 290,000 hectares of peatland forest — the world's largest tropical peatland complex, storing 30 billion tons of carbon.

cards.title: Chile Solar Energy Initiative
cards.subtitle: Atacama · Solar Power
cards.detail: Large-scale solar farm in the Atacama Desert generating 200 MW, replacing diesel generation in remote communities.

stages.title: The Global Pattern
stages.body:
Zooming out, the distribution of projects reveals a stark pattern:

🌍 **Tropical developing nations** host most nature-based projects because they hold the planet's most carbon-rich ecosystems. But they've contributed least to the problem.

📊 **The inequity:** The top 10 historical emitters produced **70%** of all industrial CO₂. Yet climate impacts fall hardest on nations least equipped to adapt.

This is why **climate justice** is central to every carbon market negotiation. Finance must flow from high-emitting to vulnerable nations.

callouts.label: Top 10 emitters share of historical CO₂

questions.question: How many verified carbon projects exist globally?
questions.explanation: As of 2026, there are 8,877 Verra and Gold Standard verified projects.

questions.question: Which ecosystem sequesters 3-5x more carbon per hectare than forests?
questions.explanation: Mangrove forests store 3-5x more carbon per unit area than terrestrial forests due to dense root systems and waterlogged soils.

questions.question: What fraction of carbon projects are in developing countries?
questions.explanation: Approximately 78% of nature-based carbon projects are located in developing countries.

questions.question: What is the climate finance gap?
questions.hint: The $100B pledge has delivered about $83B. Think about the difference.

stages.title: Projects Inspire. Markets Fund.
stages.body:
You've seen that **8,877 real projects** are fighting climate change on the ground. But how do they get funded? How are carbon credits priced, traded, and ensured to be real?

The **carbon market** is the financial system designed to do exactly that — but it's full of controversy and contradictions.
